message:
Welcome to Too Good To Be True, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

 *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin Start button
Test
*Incident Number INC100048:* Test
Clicked undefined button
Clicked org button
Clicked undefined button
Clicked Start button
Welcome our new IM @Meera Nadigadda, Happy to have you in the team. Hope you get comfortable with everyone
:alarm_clock: - You have 25 minutes to resolve the outage, good luck! You can type `/help` if you need any reminders.
Welcome @Meera Nadigadda
Nice to meet you @Meera Nadigadda
:wave: Hello, team!
There are a few strange customer reports with the helpdesk
Apparently, the site is loading slowly
People are reporting that they can't make payments either
customer_reports
@Meera Nadigadda Can you check this?
Looking into it
Same for me!
What's happening?
Customers are losing their minds online, this is NOT good
Website is unavailable for customers
*Incident Number INC100094:* Website is unavailable for customers
:star: Incident record created
:star: Send first comms in first 5 minutes
How many customers are affected? Are the reports coming in from all regions?
:star: Qualify the problem
Do you need any more details from me?

I'm having a look at it Bob , i see couple of issues in the Grafana dashboard not sure why it caused

2025/10/15 14:31:48 [error] 21#21: *174 connect() failed (111: Connection refused) while connecting to upstream, client: 100.64.50.204, server: , request: "GET /favicon.ico HTTP/1.0", upstream: "<http://172.20.233.152:80/favicon.ico>", host: "nginx-x-default-x-vilsvusp5xpdsjybvmwfvqp.vcluster-vilsvusp5xpdsjybvmwfvqp.svc.cluster.local", referrer: "<https://hipster.prod.uptimelabs.io/>"
How can I help? I see we have a problem
:star: Facts over opinion
That error is above my pay grade
I can help you with customer centric info though
:bulb: Hint: keep the business informed regularly
looks like all the customers are impacted at the moment
Do you want the deployment list?
yes Daniel
Below are the changes implemented this morning
deployments
If you want more information, check out Incident Manager dashboard for more details: <https://grafana.prod.uptimelabs.io/d/aOf5yan4z/incident-management>
I can do a deep dive on the logs also to see what's going on
yes please
Yes Boss
Looking into this and trying to replicate the issue
I am able to replicate the issue. Checking the logs now
I see a lot of errors, most of them are context initialisation errors
Logs
Yes replicated the issue and even i'm seeing similar errors in frontend logs
I have a stake holders meet in next 10 minutes, @Meera Nadigadda do you think this can be resolved by then?
there were couple of changes implemented this morning, we are thinking that could cause the issue, the team is a having a look at the logs, will let you know with all the details shortly
There are no alerts on the Infrastructure. I did see a few errors on the frontend apps
:star: Check changes done to the platform
:star: Reviewed changes
This looks to me like an issue in the frontend pod
yeah, by the looks of it
Could you please revert the changes that are causing the issue, since the business team has the meeting in the next 10 mins?
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
will see if that works
I'll need a few minutes to review the deployments first to figure out the affecting change
Or do you have a hunch as to which change is causing the problems?
looks like the environmental variables are missed while deploying and also it is unable to find the configuration file
i think that was the issue
We had 3 deployments today.. what's the change number?
otherwise I'll just investigate now if you don't know what change it is
:alarm_clock: - You have less than 4 minutes. You can type `/help` if you need any reminders.
Ooh Shay's change got it
i believe so
Sorry, I've never done a rollback in PROD before, can someone help me with the steps?
I will help you, @Shay. Rolling back now
Daniel could you please help Shay with the rollback steps?
I have rollbacked Frontend:version to 10.1.0 to more stable version. Can somebody verify?

To 'verify' that the resolution has worked and to close the incident you must: 
--Validate the fix yourself or with the team:white_check_mark: 
--Cross check resolution with Customer Support:white_check_mark: 
--Confirm resolution in the 'Business-comms' channel:white_check_mark:
Customers are able to place the orders, complaint calls have reduced as well.
:alarm_clock: - You have less than 2 minutes. You can type `/help` if you need any reminders.
It's definitely working again!
Wow that was intense!
Thanks! for resolving it quickly
The Issues hass been identified and fixed, the Website is now available to use, sorry for any inconvienece
Good job guys
Yes well done! that could have gone much worse so props to everyone
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
We will have a Post-Incident Review (PIR) to understand what happened

Thank you for playing, We recommend that you take 5 minutes to go and get a drink or stretch your legs.
Once you are back we ask that you complete this retrospective and feedback form, just hit I am ready when you want to start. I am ready button
Clicked Start_feedback button
Submission successful
